summary:
The epigenetic status of introns can indeed impact gene expression; they serve as hosts for microRNAs and harbor histone modifications linked to alternative splicing decisions, both contributing directly to transcriptome regulation.

\boxed{A}

The epigenetic status of introns influences gene expression through mechanisms such as housing microRNA precursors and undergoing histone modifications that guide RNA splicing alternatives. These findings support a role beyond traditional promoter-centric views, demonstrating active involvement of intronic epigenetics in regulating downstream transcripts.

Epigenetic markers within introns contribute to gene regulation by facilitating microRNA biogenesis and modulating alternative splicing through histone modifications.
